qa_method.name: GCN over QA reachability graph (modulus-sweep boundary test)
qa_method.description: Same two-layer plain-torch GCN as benchmark v2 (qa_ml_orbit_classifier_gnn_v2), now run across moduli m in {9, 12, 15, 18, 21, 24, 27, 30, 36}. For each m, nodes = full {1,...,m}^2 grid; node features = qa_packet_full (b, e, d, a, C, F, G, b mod (m//3), e mod (m//3)); edges = applications of sigma, mu, lambda_2, nu where defined; adjacency is symmetrized and normalized D^(-1/2)(A+I)D^(-1/2). Labels are derived from qa_orbit_rules.orbit_period: period 1 -> singularity (masked); period 8 -> satellite (1); else -> cosmos (0). Algebraic rule from orbit_family is recorded for agreement comparison.
qa_method.observer_projection: Integer (b, e) discrete state -> integer feature packet -> float64 normalized adjacency -> torch GCN -> orbit-class logits. Same Theorem NT boundary structure as v2.

datasets.name: qa_orbit_modulus_sweep
datasets.source: tools.qa_ml.qa_dataset.all_pairs(m) for m in {9, 12, 15, 18, 21, 24, 27, 30, 36}; labels via qa_orbit_rules.orbit_period; ground truth qa_orbit_rules.orbit_family for agreement check

calibration_provenance.learned_on: qa_full features deterministic; GCN init from torch default with fixed seed; per-fold z-score on train mask only; no other calibration
calibration_provenance.procedure: no calibration step beyond per-fold standardization
calibration_provenance.domain_of_origin: self-contained QA orbit grids; no inheritance

diffs: ported from single-modulus mod-24 benchmark to a 9-modulus sweep
labels switched from algebraic orbit_family to empirical orbit_period (period 1 -> singularity, period 8 -> satellite, else -> cosmos)
train fraction (0.30) replaces fixed n_train=160 to scale across grid sizes

source_mapping.primary_source: 0 errors against simulation for mod-9 and mod-24
source_mapping.mapping_rationale: QA-ML v2 showed graph topology beats feature-only baselines on mod-24. Before claiming a cert, this sweep tests whether the advantage is mod-24-specific or generalizes to other moduli where the satellite class (orbit period 8) is non-trivial. The qa_orbit_rules verification on m in {9, 24} is the primary source for the orbit-class definition; the sweep extends the test domain by classifying empirically via orbit_period and recording where algebraic and empirical labels agree.

ablation.destroyed_structure: Replace the QA-generator normalized adjacency with the identity matrix per modulus; same node features, architecture, seed. Holds everything constant except the message-passing edges, isolating the graph contribution at every m.
ablation.expected_direction: with_graph - without_graph >= +0.10 macro F1 for moduli with a non-trivial satellite class. If the advantage collapses for some m (PASS_MODULI vs FAIL_MODULI), that boundary is the cert claim's scope rather than a refutation.
